name: Agatha Christie's Poirot
overview: David Suchet plays Hercule Poirot across 70 episodes spanning the complete Christie canon — the definitive screen portrayal of the little Belgian detective.

name: Miss Marple (Joan Hickson)
overview: Joan Hickson's peerless Miss Marple solves murders across 12 feature-length BBC adaptations — still regarded as the gold standard for Christie on screen.

name: Inspector Morse
overview: The erudite, crossword-addicted Chief Inspector Morse and his loyal Sergeant Lewis investigate murders in and around Oxford — the benchmark of British crime drama.

name: Vera
overview: DCI Vera Stanhope — solitary, sharp-tongued, and brilliantly observant — investigates dark crimes across the bleak, beautiful Northumberland landscape.

name: Broadchurch
overview: A young boy is found dead on a Dorset beach and the murder investigation tears a small coastal community apart — a masterpiece of slow-burn British crime drama.

name: Planet Earth
overview: David Attenborough narrates the BBC's landmark 11-part natural history series capturing Earth's most astonishing habitats and the creatures that inhabit them.

name: Blue Planet
overview: David Attenborough explores the world's oceans from the sunlit surface to the pitch-black abyss in this landmark eight-part BBC natural history series.

name: Blue Planet II
overview: David Attenborough returns to the ocean in a new seven-part series using the latest technology to reveal breathtaking underwater worlds never seen before.

name: Midsomer Murders
overview: Chief Inspector Tom Barnaby navigates the surprisingly lethal English countryside, where picturesque village life conceals an extraordinary number of murders.

name: Grantchester
overview: An idealistic Cambridge vicar moonlights as a reluctant amateur sleuth alongside his detective friend — a warm, beautifully shot 1950s English mystery series.

name: Planet Earth II
overview: David Attenborough returns to reveal a dramatically changed planet in six landmark episodes — jungles, mountains, grasslands, deserts, islands, and cities.

name: Doc Martin
overview: A brilliant but brusque London surgeon develops a blood phobia and retreats to a tiny Cornish village as a GP — charming, warm, and wickedly funny.

name: Endeavour
overview: Young Constable Morse navigates Oxford's class divide and his own complex inner life while solving murders in this exquisitely crafted Inspector Morse prequel.

name: Lewis
overview: Newly promoted Inspector Robbie Lewis and his intellectually brilliant sergeant solve complex murders in contemporary Oxford — the affectionate Morse spin-off.

name: Wild Isles
overview: David Attenborough celebrates the remarkable wildlife of Britain and Ireland across five episodes — the most intimate nature documentary ever made about the British Isles.

name: Sherlock
overview: A brilliantly modern reimagining of Arthur Conan Doyle's detective, set in contemporary London with Benedict Cumberbatch and Martin Freeman — sharp, stylish, and intelligent.

name: Our Planet
overview: David Attenborough narrates this Netflix landmark series showing the remarkable diversity of life on Earth and the urgency of protecting it for future generations.

name: Father Brown
overview: The unassuming Catholic priest and amateur detective solves the most perplexing crimes in 1950s rural England — cosy, gently humorous, and deeply satisfying.

name: Downton Abbey
overview: The lives of the aristocratic Crawley family and their devoted servants intertwine across two decades of sweeping British history — the beloved period drama.

name: Frozen Planet
overview: David Attenborough leads viewers to the last great wildernesses at the top and bottom of the Earth in seven stunning episodes about life at the poles.
